title: The Criminal Trade In Ancient Objects

blanks.sentence: Purchased a Roman coin aged _____
blanks.evidence: "the 1,800-year-old Roman coin which BBC reporter Ryan Keane recently bought in London"

blanks.sentence: Research revealed extensive illegal networks involving _____
blanks.evidence: "I came across reports of expansive criminal networks related to antiquities"

blanks.sentence: Many historical items sold have been taken by force and _____
blanks.evidence: "Some ancient artefacts being sold online and in stores have been looted and trafficked"

blanks.sentence: Objects created by humans with historical value, e.g. coins and _____
blanks.evidence: "which is historically interesting – for example, a coin or statue"

blanks.sentence: During _____, items were commonly removed from their places of origin
blanks.evidence: "During colonial times, it was common for artefacts to be taken"

blanks.sentence: Relocated to _____ and museums in other countries
blanks.evidence: "moved to museums and private collections abroad"

blanks.sentence: _____ has transformed the illegal trade
blanks.evidence: "Social media is the game changer here"

blanks.sentence: Previously, sellers needed to work with _____ to find buyers
blanks.evidence: "you had to go through intermediaries"

blanks.sentence: This difficulty acted as a _____ in the process
blanks.evidence: "this problem, or bottleneck, is gone"

blanks.sentence: Now communication between sellers and _____ is direct
blanks.evidence: "communicate with someone on the demand side"

blanks.sentence: Museums serve as the _____ destination for many stolen items
blanks.evidence: "as the final destination for much looted treasure"

blanks.sentence: Several institutions, such as the Louvre in _____, face allegations
blanks.evidence: "including the Louvre in Paris"

blanks.sentence: Accusations include purchasing stolen items either knowingly or _____
blanks.evidence: "Accusations of knowingly or unknowingly buying stolen objects"

blanks.sentence: Victoria Reed works as _____ _____ at the institution
blanks.evidence: "Victoria Reed is senior curator at the Museum of Fine Arts"
blanks.section: Case study: Museum of Fine Arts

blanks.sentence: In 2024, the museum returned a necklace taken illegally from _____
blanks.evidence: "which had been taken illegally from Turkey"
blanks.section: Case study: Museum of Fine Arts

blanks.sentence: The theft occurred during the _____
blanks.evidence: "from Turkey in the 1970s"
blanks.section: Case study: Museum of Fine Arts

blanks.sentence: Returning items to their _____ is called repatriation
blanks.evidence: "returning artefacts to their country of origin"

blanks.sentence: Victoria Reed supports practice of _____
blanks.evidence: "I believe in responsible collecting"

blanks.sentence: She also advocates for careful _____ decisions
blanks.evidence: "I believe in judicious repatriation decisions"

blanks.sentence: Her position is considered not extreme and acceptable – a _____ stance
blanks.evidence: "Victoria takes a middle-of-the-road approach"

multipleChoice.question: What attitude does the speaker Ryan Keane express towards the trade in ancient artefacts?
options: A. He is alarmed by the extent of criminal networks involved
B. He is doubtful about the authenticity of ancient objects
C. He is indifferent to how artefacts are sold online
D. He is excited about finding new collectors for artefacts
multipleChoice.evidence: Ryan说 "I came across reports of expansive criminal networks related to antiquities"（第8行），表明他对非法网络规模感到关注。

multipleChoice.question: According to Neil, what is the main reason ancient artefacts are historically interesting?
options: A. They are objects crafted by humans long ago
B. They are valuable due to their material worth
C. They are made in famous museums worldwide
D. They are always linked to famous movie stories
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil定义artefact为“any object made by humans, usually very old”（第11-12行）。

multipleChoice.question: What does the word "looted" most likely refer to in this context?
options: A. Objects stolen by force or illegal means
B. Items carefully excavated by archaeologists
C. Museums purchasing objects through legal channels
D. Artefacts displayed with full government approval
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth解释“looted – or stolen by force”（第17行）。

multipleChoice.question: What does Amr Al-Azm mean by calling social media a “game changer” for illegal artefact sales?
options: A. Social media destroys the market for stolen items
B. Social media facilitates easy communication between sellers and buyers
C. Social media increases the difficulty of selling artefacts
D. Social media forces middlemen to act as barriers
multipleChoice.evidence: Amr称“Social media is the game changer... it just opens it up”（第23-24行），强调沟通便捷。

multipleChoice.question: What is the main problem that "bottleneck" describes in this programme?
options: A. It is the delay in returning artefacts to countries
B. It is the blockage that slows down seller-buyer contact
C. It is the shortage of ancient objects on the market
D. It is the museum’s refusal to display looted artefacts
multipleChoice.evidence: “A bottleneck is a problem that delays a process or stops it from progressing”（第26-27行），前文提到之前沟通难是瓶颈。

multipleChoice.question: What role do museums play in the fight against illegal artefact trading?
options: A. They are the final destinations for most looted treasures
B. They sell stolen objects to private collectors
C. They ignore accusations related to illegal purchases
D. They avoid returning artefacts to original countries
multipleChoice.evidence: “museums have an important role... final destination for much looted treasure”（第29-30行）

multipleChoice.question: How does Victoria Reed describe her approach to repatriation decisions?
options: A. She supports only returning artefacts upon strong evidence
B. She takes a moderate view, balancing responsibility and caution
C. She opposes repatriation completely to keep artefacts in museums
D. She believes repatriation should always happen immediately
multipleChoice.evidence: Victoria说“middle-of-the-road approach... responsible collecting... judicious repatriation decisions”（第33-35行）

multipleChoice.question: What does the phrase “middle-of-the-road” mean in this discussion?
options: A. A position that is extreme and controversial
B. A balanced viewpoint acceptable to most people
C. A decision made without careful consideration
D. An opinion favored only by museum curators
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth解释middle-of-the-road是“not extreme and is acceptable to most people”（第35-37行）

multipleChoice.question: What ongoing debate is mentioned regarding ancient artefacts?
options: A. Whether they should be preserved or destroyed over time
B. If objects should be returned to their origin or stay in museums
C. How to increase the sale of artefacts on social media
D. Whether ancient objects are authentic or modern fakes
multipleChoice.evidence: 文末提到“whether artefacts find... back to their original homes or displayed in museums remains an ongoing debate”（第39-40行）

multipleChoice.question: Why is the discovery of the Mask of Tutankhamun considered important?
options: A. It is a famous ancient artefact found in Egypt in 1922
B. It was the first item stolen from the Valley of the Kings
C. It was originally kept in private collections abroad
D. It was used as a prop in popular adventure movies
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil问答确认“the Mask of Tutankhamun”于1922发现（第5行，后续）
